title: Left Atrial Appendage Closure or Anticoagulation for Atrial Fibrillation
one_line_summary: In patients with atrial fibrillation eligible for anticoagulation, left atrial appendage closure was noninferior to NOACs for preventing cardiovascular death, stroke, or systemic embolism and **superior** for reducing non-procedure-related bleeding at 3 years.
key_pearls: Device-based left atrial appendage closure (LAAC) with Watchman Flx was noninferior to NOAC therapy for the composite of **cardiovascular death, stroke, or systemic embolism** at 3 years (5.7% vs 4.8%; difference 0.9 percentage points; 95% CI −0.8 to 2.6; P<0.001).

LAAC was **superior** to NOAC therapy for **non-procedure-related bleeding** at 3 years (10.9% vs 19.0%; HR 0.55; 95% CI 0.45 to 0.67; P<0.001), driven mainly by clinically relevant nonmajor bleeding.
The annualized rate of **ischemic stroke or systemic embolism** was 1.1% per year in the device group versus 0.8% per year in the NOAC group, a 0.3% **absolute** annual increase.
Successful device implantation was achieved in **92.5%** of patients, with effective closure (residual leak ≤3 mm) in **98.6%** at 4 months.
Device-related thrombus was detected in **4.8%** of patients at 4 months, with clinically relevant thrombus requiring anticoagulation resumption in **1.8%**.
Post-implantation antithrombotic regimen included **3 months** of NOAC plus aspirin, NOAC monotherapy, or dual antiplatelet therapy, followed by aspirin or P2Y12 inhibitor monotherapy.
The trial population had a mean **CHA2DS2-VASc score of 3.5** and a mean **HAS-BLED score of 1.3**, representing a moderate-risk population underrepresented in the highest-risk strata (score ≥5).
population: Patients with atrial fibrillation at increased risk for stroke (CHA2DS2-VASc score ≥2 for men, ≥3 for women) who were suitable candidates for long-term NOAC therapy. Mean age 71.7 years, 31.9% women.

sections.heading: Background
sections.content:
- Atrial fibrillation is the most common sustained arrhythmia, affecting an estimated **10.5 million** persons in the US and **60 million** worldwide.
- A major complication of atrial fibrillation is cardioembolic stroke.
- NOACs are the preferred treatment for stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation, but uptake and adherence are limited by bleeding complications.
- Left atrial appendage closure (LAAC) devices are currently approved for patients with a rationale to avoid long-term oral anticoagulation.
- The benefit of LAAC has not been well studied in patients who are suitable candidates for NOACs.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.content:
- **Trial Design:** CHAMPION-AF is an ongoing, prospective, international, randomized, open-label trial sponsored by Boston Scientific.
- **Randomization:** 3000 patients were randomly assigned in a 1:1 ratio to receive a Watchman Flx device (device group) or an approved NOAC (anticoagulation group).
- **Key Inclusion Criteria:** Atrial fibrillation with CHA2DS2-VASc score ≥2 (men) or ≥3 (women); suitable for long-term NOAC therapy.
- **Key Exclusion Criteria:** Myocardial infarction, stroke, or TIA within 30 days before enrollment; major bleeding event (ISTH criteria) within 30 days.
- **Device Group Protocol:** Device implantation within 14 days of randomization; post-implant antithrombotic regimen of NOAC plus aspirin, NOAC monotherapy, or dual antiplatelet therapy for **3 months**, followed by aspirin or P2Y12 inhibitor monotherapy.
- **Primary Efficacy End Point:** Composite of death from cardiovascular causes, stroke, or systemic embolism at 3 years, tested for noninferiority (margin **4.8 percentage points**).
- **Primary Safety End Point:** Non-procedure-related bleeding (ISTH major + clinically relevant nonmajor bleeding) at 3 years, tested for superiority.
- **Secondary End Points:** Procedure-related and non-procedure-related ISTH major bleeding (noninferiority); net clinical benefit composite (noninferiority margin **9.6 percentage points**).
- **Statistical Power:** 90% power to show noninferiority assuming 12% event rate and 12.5% attrition; hierarchical testing strategy to control multiplicity.
section_pearls: The noninferiority margin for the primary efficacy end point was set at **4.8 percentage points** on an absolute scale, corresponding to a relative risk of 1.4.

sections.heading: Results
sections.content:
- **Patient Disposition:** 1499 assigned to device group, 1501 to anticoagulation group; 92.5% received the device; 3-year follow-up completion was 92.7% (device) and 86.9% (anticoagulation).
- **Baseline Characteristics:** Mean age **71.7±7.5 years**; 31.9% women; mean CHA2DS2-VASc score **3.5±1.3**; mean HAS-BLED score **1.3±0.8**; 68.9% paroxysmal AF; 47.8% prior AF ablation.
- **Primary Efficacy End Point:** Occurred in 81 patients (5.7%) in the device group vs 65 patients (4.8%) in the anticoagulation group (difference **0.9 percentage points**; 95% CI −0.8 to 2.6; P<0.001 for noninferiority; HR 1.20; 95% CI 0.87 to 1.66).
- **Primary Safety End Point:** Non-procedure-related bleeding occurred in 154 patients (10.9%) in the device group vs 260 patients (19.0%) in the anticoagulation group (difference **−8.1 percentage points**; 95% CI −10.8 to −5.5; P<0.001 for superiority; HR 0.55; 95% CI 0.45 to 0.67).
- **Secondary Safety End Point (ISTH Major Bleeding):** 5.9% device vs 6.4% anticoagulation (difference −0.6 percentage points; P<0.001 for noninferiority).
- **Net Clinical Benefit:** 15.1% device vs 21.8% anticoagulation (difference −6.7 percentage points; P<0.001 for noninferiority; HR 0.66; 95% CI 0.56 to 0.79).
- **Stroke Outcomes:** Ischemic stroke or systemic embolism occurred in 3.2% (device) vs 2.2% (anticoagulation); hemorrhagic stroke in 0.4% in both groups.
- **Procedural Safety:** Pericardial effusion requiring intervention within 30 days occurred in **0.7%** of the device group.
- **Device-Related Thrombus:** Detected in 63 of 1320 patients (4.8%) at 4 months; 24 patients (1.8%) had clinically relevant thrombus requiring resumption of anticoagulation; 2 had a stroke.
section_pearls: LAAC reduced non-procedure-related bleeding by **42%** (HR 0.55) compared to NOACs, primarily driven by a reduction in clinically relevant nonmajor bleeding.
The absolute difference in ischemic stroke or systemic embolism was **1.0 percentage point** over 3 years, favoring NOACs.

sections.heading: Discussion
sections.content:
- LAAC was noninferior to NOACs for the composite efficacy end point and **superior** for reducing bleeding in a population eligible for anticoagulation.
- The composite end point aligns with patient-important sequelae of atrial fibrillation.
- The annualized rate of ischemic stroke or systemic embolism was **1.1%** per year in the device group, comparable to **1%** per year observed with apixaban in the ARISTOTLE trial.
- The between-group difference in ischemic stroke or systemic embolism was **0.3%** per year; the ongoing 5-year follow-up will assess persistence of this difference.
- Clinically relevant nonmajor bleeding events often prompt discontinuation of anticoagulation, highlighting the clinical significance of the bleeding reduction with LAAC.
- Post hoc analysis showed that after **180 days**, bleeding events continued to diverge in favor of the device group.
- CT angiography use increased during the COVID-19 pandemic, increasing sensitivity for detecting hypoattenuated thickening, which may overestimate clinically **significant** device-related thrombus.
- Peridevice leak: 98.6% had effective closure (≤3 mm), but controversy exists regarding the stroke risk associated with any residual leak.
- Net clinical benefit analysis supports LAAC as a reasonable alternative to NOACs in shared decision-making.
section_pearls: The trial population had a lower-than-expected event rate, making the absolute noninferiority margin potentially permissive of a higher relative risk.
Results may not be generalizable to patients with CHA2DS2-VASc score ≥5, who were underrepresented.

strengths_limitations:
- **Strengths:** Large, international, randomized trial design; rigorous hierarchical statistical testing plan; independent clinical event adjudication and core laboratory imaging assessment; addresses a clinically relevant knowledge gap for patients eligible for anticoagulation.
- **Limitations:** Noninferiority margins used an **absolute** scale, allowing a potentially higher relative risk given the lower-than-expected event rate; only one LAAC device (Watchman Flx) was studied, limiting generalizability to other devices; patients with CHA2DS2-VASc score ≥5 were underrepresented; majority of patients had paroxysmal AF and prior ablation, which may not reflect all AF populations; open-label design may introduce bias in bleeding event reporting.

drugs_doses.dose: Per standard clinical guidelines; specific agents and doses not mandated by protocol
drugs_doses.indication: Stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation for patients in the anticoagulation group
drugs_doses.adverse_effects: Non-procedure-related ISTH major bleeding (6.4% at 3 years); clinically relevant nonmajor bleeding (14.2% at 3 years)

drugs_doses.indication: Post-LAAC antithrombotic regimen to prevent device-related thrombus
drugs_doses.adverse_effects: Bleeding risk; part of post-implantation regimen

drugs_doses.dose: Monotherapy after 3 months post-implantation (alternative to aspirin)
drugs_doses.indication: Post-LAAC antithrombotic regimen to prevent device-related thrombus
drugs_doses.adverse_effects: Bleeding risk; part of post-implantation regimen
